faculty.treePosition: Start of the Tree (Paut Neteru) sequence — practiced first, before moving down to the other ten faculties.
teachingNotes: AMEN is the foundation of all other faculties as it is the inexhaustible reservoir of INNER PEACE that nourishes our Vitality, Health, and activates the Divine Faculties of Oneness, Wisdom, and Spiritual Power.

Start your meditation practice with the AMEN faculty, moving down the Tree in order, ending with Geb. Where you have a spiritual reading or some other directive to work urgently on one of the other faculties, it is best to combine your meditation with AMEN and the other faculty, unless your previous spiritual work has already established this foundation.

Ideally, you will work on one to two faculties per month in 21 days of meditation and 7 days of review and preparation for the next cycle. At this pace, you will complete your first journey through the Tree in six to twelve months.

hekau.text: I am a divine spiritual being endowed with the ability to respond to all challenges in a peaceful way to guarantee the wellbeing of everyone. Peace protects my health, uplifts my intelligence, and guarantees me a successful destiny.
You could have made us incapable of being at peace in the midst of challenges, like dogs, and pigs. I thank you God, for making us in your likeness.
Thank you for the peace that you placed in the core of my being. It blesses me with the ability to live truth on the inside, as I live it on the outside. Emblem of sincerity.

Since it has been medically proven for thousands of years that indulging anger, fear, worry, grief, and sadness damages well-being, lowers IQ, life performances, and social harmony, I endure in my efforts to always respond with peace to all challenges. I am a divine spiritual being.

Although many people cannot intuit the truth that the universe was created by an all-knowing, and powerful being, and seeing that the world has been designed with interest in the well-being of all, I place my trust in this being God, the Supreme Being, for my wellness in life. I know that it will come to pass because of living the Laws of God. I am therefore eternally at peace in all situations.
It is with smiles, and laughter that I enter the kingdom of doom, and disaster. Peace is my nature. What good is there in losing it?
You saved me from the tyranny of suffering before I was born. You made me peaceful by nature.
I take neither gain, or loss to heart. Peace is my nature.
I give thanks for the wrongdoer that has crossed my path to give me a genuine opportunity to nourish my divine nature that I may realize my divinity.

breathingTechnique.summary: Slow the breathing rate from the customary 15-18 breaths per minute to about 7 breaths per minute (roughly 4 seconds in, 4 seconds out).
breathingTechnique.inbreath: Gently push out the lower abdomen during the inbreath, and at the end apply a gentle tension in the outstretched abdomen.
breathingTechnique.outbreath: The tension is gently maintained throughout the out-breath, in which the lower abdomen is pulled back in.
breathingTechnique.principle: Lower abdominal tension stimulates the vagus nerve, energizing the waking center of the brain and the will. Mental wandering during meditation comes from diminished wakefulness caused by the lowered breathing rate; pressure on the vagus nerve counteracts this.

environmentProtocols: Meditate in a clean, uncluttered area.
The room should be free of odors such as food or cleaning materials. Lightly scent the room with incense of Olibanum (Frankincense) or Sweet Basil.
Set up a small table displaying elements associated with the objectives of the meditation (peace, love, serenity, your success goals) — things that must be in your line of sight.
Sit in a chair that gives proper support to your back, kept upright yet relaxed. Never slump, never sit in a tense manner.
meditationInstructions.expectedSensations: Deepening calm and relaxation, effortless or day-dreamy thought flow, a feeling of detachment, and/or bodily sensations of warmth, coolness, tingling, light electrical sensations, or the body feeling very heavy, very light, or drifting — these are normal. Pain or congestion may indicate posture/breathing issues; if persistent, may indicate blockages addressed through yoga, pranayama, Qi Gong, acupuncture/acupressure, or massage therapy.

scriptSteps.name: Trigger scenario
scriptSteps.instruction: Recall or visualize concretely a scenario that habitually triggers a negative response in you (do not include the negative response itself in the script — only the trigger).

scriptSteps.name: Affirm the law
scriptSteps.instruction: Affirm the law that governs the spiritual faculty of the situation — for Amen, a peace-based affirmation — replacing the negative internal chatter that feeds the incorrect response.

scriptSteps.name: Visualize living the law
scriptSteps.instruction: Visualize yourself smiling or laughing in the situation, joyfully continuing to engage rather than reacting negatively. Outward or inward smiling/laughter is described as releasing endorphins and mood-enhancing neurochemicals that counter stress.

scriptSteps.name: Visualize the blessing and give thanks
scriptSteps.instruction: Visualize yourself enjoying the successful realization of a worldly goal (relationship harmony, wealth, health) as an effect of living the law. Conclude by acknowledging to God any failures to live the law that day, pledging to do better, and giving thanks.

meditationInstructions.durationGuidance: Perform for 21 consecutive days for the behavioral change to take full effect, followed by a nightly accounting/recommitment step. 20-30 minutes per session; ideally done as the last thing before bed.

journalPrompts: What negative emotional response (anger, worry, fear, grief, sadness) came up for you today, and what specifically triggered it?
Rank today's trigger by frequency and seriousness to your wellbeing compared to other recurring triggers.
Describe, in vivid detail, the trigger scenario you want to use in tomorrow's meditation script.
Which Amen affirmation did you use today, and did it come to mind automatically in the moment of challenge, or only afterward?
Where did you fall short of responding with peace today? Acknowledge it, and note your recommitment for tomorrow.
suggestedDailyActions: Complete one 20-30 minute Amen meditation session using the breathing technique and the 4-step script.
Select or compose one Amen affirmation to carry with you through the day.
Log today's practice in the tracker (trigger, affirmation used, visualization, outcome).
If you missed the full meditation, repeat the script several times before moving into your day rather than skipping it entirely.
